headlines.title: Energy Security Concerns Force the EU to Reconsider Domestic Gas Drilling
headlines.summary: The energy ministers of European Union members are set to discuss local gas drilling in response to the energy supply squeeze in the Middle East, Reuters has reported, citing an internal document it had seen. Domestic gas drilling has not been particularly popular among European

headlines.title: Australia Plans $7 Billion Fuel Stockpile
headlines.summary: Australia’s government plans to spend A$10 billion, or $7 billion, on building its fuel stock to avoid future supply squeezes. The plans involve stocking up enough to cover at least 50 days of demand, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said, as quoted by Reuters today. This means a

headlines.title: Oil Futures Markets Still Too Complacent About Supply Shock
headlines.summary: The oil futures market appears not to have caught up with the massive supply disruption from the Middle East yet—a supply shock that is now in its third month and lasting more than anyone expected in early March. headlines.title: U.S. Oil Can’t Fill the Middle East Supply Hole
headlines.summary: The United States is exporting crude at record levels as the world grapples with an unprecedented disruption of Middle Eastern supply. headlines.title: UAE’s Shock Exit Leaves OPEC Weakened and the Global Oil Order Rewritten
headlines.summary: Before 1 May, the once-mighty Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) had 12 members, but with the departure of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) at the start of the month, it has 11. headlines.title: API Confirms Very Large Crude and Oil Product Draws
headlines.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 8.1 million barrels in the week ending May 1. headlines.title: Venezuela’s Oil Rebound Shows Why the Petrodollar Is a Logistics System
headlines.summary: Venezuela’s oil is moving again. That does not mean Venezuela’s oil industry has been rebuilt.
